#### Thank you [...] ### ChatGPTRate this definition:0.0 / 0 votes

knockoff

A knockoff is a cheaper imitation or copy of a popular or high-priced product or item, often of inferior quality. This term is most commonly used to describe items such as designer clothes, accessories, and electronics that are replicated and sold at lower prices, often violating copyright or trademark laws.

### Matched Categories

### What does knockoff mean?

# Definitions for knockoff knock·off

#### This dictionary definitions page includes all the possible meanings, example usage and translations of the word knockoff.

### Princeton's WordNetRate this definition:0.0 / 0 votes

knockoff, clonenoun

an unauthorized copy or imitation

### WiktionaryRate this definition:0.0 / 0 votes

knockoffnoun

An imitation of something, particularly a well-known product, usually lower in quality and price than the original.

It's not a name brand bag, just a cheap knockoff.

# Package: knockoff 0.3.6

Matteo Sesia

## knockoff: The Knockoff Filter for Controlled Variable Selection

The knockoff filter is a general procedure for controlling the false discovery rate (FDR) when performing variable selection. For more information, see the website below and the accompanying paper: Candes et al., "Panning for gold: model-X knockoffs for high-dimensional controlled variable selection", J. R. Statist. Soc. B (2018) 80, 3, pp. 551-577.

Authors:Rina Foygel Barber [ctb], Emmanuel Candes [ctb], Lucas Janson [ctb], Evan Patterson [aut], Matteo Sesia [aut, cre] [...] ## Usage by other packages (reverse dependencies)

without having to keep inventory so how this works is you're going to find a really good vendor and once someone purchases from your website or purchases from you directly you would then take those definitely going to determine the success of your [...] is that you want to get a business card you can do PayPal business or you can order a cash app card if you're selling through DM you can use a cash app card venmo has cards as well so once the customer places the order with you you're going to use the funds from that card you're able to access them directly with these debit cards and you're going to place your order with the vendor and give the vendor your customer's address and then the vendor is going to ship that order directly to your customer so it's like you're pretty much playing the middleman but you are upcharging if your vendor is selling a handbag for $50 and you're marking that handbag up to $150 you would be making $100 profit of course you would include whatever fees
